tool.name: chatGPT

secret.name: openai_api_key
secret.label: OpenAI API Key
secret.description: Required when using the OpenAI server type. You can obtain it from your OpenAI account dashboard.

credentials.name: custom_server_credentials
credentials.label: Custom Server Credentials
credentials.description: Credentials for accessing a custom OpenAI-compatible server.

variable.name: custom_server_url
variable.label: Server URL
variable.description: Base URL of the OpenAI-compatible API (e.g. http://localhost:8000/v1, https://my-server.com/v1).

secret.name: custom_server_api_key
secret.label: Custom Server API Key
secret.description: Only needed if your custom server requires authentication. Leave empty for servers without API key requirements (e.g., local vLLM or Ollama).

tool.command:
#import json #import os #import re #set LINK_LIST = [] #if $context #for $input in $context #set file_name = os.path.splitext($input.element_identifier)[0] ## list of supported filetypes in OpenAI. If Galaxy has a filetype that is not in this list, just use the generic `txt`. #set ext = $input.ext if $input.ext in ['c', 'cpp', 'css', 'csv', 'docx', 'gif', 'html', 'java', 'jpeg', 'jpg', 'js', 'json', 'md', 'pdf', 'php', 'pkl', 'png', 'pptx', 'py', 'rb', 'tar', 'tex', 'ts', 'txt', 'webp', 'xlsx', 'xml', 'zip'] else 'txt' #set LINK = re.sub('[^\w\-]', '_', $file_name)+'.'+$ext ln -s '$input' '$LINK' && ## OpenAI has some special handling of "images", so let's annotate this here and pass it to the script #set type = 'image' if $input.ext in ['jpg', 'jpeg', 'png', 'webp', 'gif'] else 'text' $LINK_LIST.append([$LINK, $type]) #end for #end if #set context_files = json.dumps($LINK_LIST)
## Resolve model based on server type #if $server_type.server_type_selection == 'openai' #set model = $server_type.model #else #set model = $server_type.custom_model #end if
python '$__tool_directory__/chatgpt.py' '$context_files' '$prompt' '$model' '$server_type.server_type_selection' '$advanced.temperature' '$advanced.max_tokens' '$advanced.top_p' '$advanced.system_message'

param.name: server_type_selection
param.label: Server
param.help: Choose where to send your request.

param.name: model
param.label: Model
param.help: Select the OpenAI model you want to use.
param.option: Flagship model for coding, reasoning, and agentic tasks across domains. (gpt-5)
Faster, more cost-efficient version of GPT-5. It's great for well-defined tasks and precise prompts. (gpt-5-mini)
Fastest, cheapest version of GPT-5. It's great for summarization and classification tasks. (gpt-5-nano)
Excels at instruction following and tool calling, with broad knowledge across domains. (gpt-4.1)
High-intelligence flagship model for complex, multi-step tasks. (gpt-4o)

param.name: custom_model
param.label: Model
param.help: Model identifier as recognized by the server (e.g. llama-3.2-8b, gpt-4o).

param.name: context
param.label: Context
param.help: Optional context data that will be uploaded for processing.

param.name: prompt
param.label: Prompt
param.help: Prompts or tasks you want the model to perform.

param.name: temperature
param.label: Temperature
param.help: Controls randomness in the output. Lower values make output more focused and deterministic, while higher values make it more creative and random. If not set, the model's default temperature will be used.

param.name: max_tokens
param.label: Max tokens
param.help: Maximum number of tokens in the response. If not set, the model's default is used.

param.name: top_p
param.label: Top P
param.help: Nucleus sampling threshold. If not set, the model's default is used.

param.name: system_message
param.label: System message
param.help: Optional system prompt to set the model's behavior. If not set, no system message is sent.

has_text.text: OpenAI API key is not provided in credentials!
Custom server URL is not provided in credentials!
Custom server URL is not provided in credentials!

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**What it does**
This tool leverages OpenAI-compatible APIs to generate responses based on user-provided context and prompts. Users can upload context data in various formats and ask questions or execute prompts related to that data. The tool processes the data using the selected model and returns an AI-generated response tailored to the context provided.
**Server Types**
You can choose from two server types:
- **OpenAI (official API)**: Connect directly to OpenAI's API using your OpenAI API key. Select from the available OpenAI models. - **Custom OpenAI-compatible server**: Connect to any server that implements the OpenAI Chat Completions API (e.g., vLLM, Ollama, LiteLLM proxy, Mistral, local deployments). The server URL and API key are configured in the Custom Server Credentials section. Only the model name is specified per run.
**Credentials**
- **OpenAI API Key**: Required when using the OpenAI server type. Enter your key in the Galaxy credentials section. - **Custom Server URL**: Required when using the Custom server type. The base URL of the OpenAI-compatible API (e.g. ``https://api.example.com/v1``). - **Custom Server API Key**: Optional. Only needed if your custom server requires authentication. Leave empty for servers without API key requirements (e.g., local vLLM or Ollama).
**Usage**
**Input**
1. **Select a Server Type**: Choose between OpenAI or Custom.
2. **Upload Context Data** (Optional): You can optionally upload up to 500 files in formats such as DOCX, HTML, JSON, PDF, TXT, JPG, PNG, or GIF. This context data serves as the input for the prompt you wish to execute. If no context is provided, the model will respond based solely on the prompt.
3. **Provide a Prompt**: Provide a prompt or task for the model to execute. The more specific the prompt, the more tailored the response will be.
**Advanced Options**
- **Temperature**: Controls randomness in the output (range: 0.0 to 2.0). Lower values make output more focused and deterministic, while higher values make it more creative and random. If not set, the model uses its default temperature.
- **Max tokens**: Maximum number of tokens in the response. If not set, the model's default is used.
- **Top P**: Nucleus sampling threshold. If not set, the model's default is used.
- **System message**: Optional system prompt to set the model's behavior (e.g., "You are a helpful biology assistant"). If not set, no system message is sent.
**Output**
The output is a response generated by the selected model, crafted based on the provided context data and the prompt. This response is saved in a Markdown file.
